steps.title: Use cubic regression
steps.content: The expression has a $c^3$ term, a $c$ term, and a constant. Desmos can fit it to $ac^3+bc^2+cc+d$ using four input values.

steps.title: Enter the regression rows
steps.content:
Use a neutral input list name so it does not conflict with the variable $c$ in the problem:
$$t_1=[-1,0,1,2]$$
$$v_1=(3t_1+9)-(4t_1^3-5)$$
$$v_1\sim at_1^3+bt_1^2+ct_1+d$$

steps.title: Read the coefficients
steps.content:
The regression gives $a=-4$, $b=0$, $c=3$, and $d=14$. So the equivalent expression is:
$$-4c^3+3c+14$$

steps.title: Confirm the subtraction
steps.content: Distributing the subtraction gives $3c+9-4c^3+5=-4c^3+3c+14$. That matches choice B, so B is correct.
